utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 top-level items. Now check if each is fully specified with all required details: 1) health_coverage: The format asks for 'physical and mental health insurance with costs.' The plan provides separate entries for physical and mental health (for user and adult children) with monthly costs specified. Although it includes extra subitems (executive wellness, specialist access, emergency mental health) with costs, the core requirement is satisfied and costs are present. Considered fully specified. 2) property_coverage: The format asks for 'home/contents insurance with costs.' The plan provides 'home_contents' with a cost and several related protections (personal collection, luxury items, hobby equipment, etc.) all with costs. This satisfies the requirement and is fully specified. 3) travel_coverage: The format asks for 'travel insurance with costs.' The plan provides 'PrimeTravel Premium Plan - €115/month.' Fully specified. 4) additional_services: The format asks for 'any enhancement services with costs.' The plan lists two services with costs (one monthly, one one-time). Fully specified. 5) total_budget: The format asks for 'complete monthly/annual cost breakdown.' The plan provides monthly cost (€1,295) and one-time fees (€300). It does not include an annual figure, but since it provides a cost breakdown (monthly plus one-time), this can reasonably be considered a complete breakdown in some sense. However, the phrase 'complete monthly/annual cost breakdown' implies both monthly and annual totals should be present. The annual cost is missing, so this item is not fully specified according to the required details. Therefore, 4 out of 5 items are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: We match each item named in the final coverage plan to the exact item names present in the ground-truth ratings. For health: LifeFirst Standard Plan appears under both physical health and mental health with ratings 8. MedSecure Basic Plan appears under both physical and mental health with rating 3. For property: PrimeHome Premium Coverage is listed with rating 10; HomeCo Basic Coverage has rating 2. The plan also lists Personal Collection Coverage, Luxury Item Protection, Hobby Equipment Coverage, Entertainment System Discount, which do not exist verbatim in the ground truth; among these, only Entertainment System Discount matches a property_enhancement_service item with rating 8. For travel: PrimeTravel Premium Plan matches with rating 10. For additional services: Premium Insurance Membership and Insurance Portfolio Management match general_services with rating 10. Executive wellness programs (Wellness Programs), Specialist Access, and Emergency Mental Health Care map to health_enhancement_services with ratings 10, 10, and 8 respectively.
